2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eForge Studio is a self-hosted AI lab control center. It acts as a proxy layer that sits between your applications and LLM providers (OpenAI, Groq, Gemini, Mistral, Ollama, llama.cpp, and others). You configure your API keys once, and Forge Studio handles routing, fallbacks, budget tracking, and monitoring.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe key idea: you paste any OpenAI-compatible API URL and key into Forge Studio, and it works. No vendor lock-in. No complex configuration. AI Lab Hub — Unified Model Catalog\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-0-ai-lab-hub--unified-model-catalog\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 0. AI Lab Hub — Unified Model Catalog\" href=\"#0-ai-lab-hub--unified-model-catalog\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe central command center for all AI models. One page replaces five: Model Manager, Model Explorer, Custom Providers, LLM Discoverer, and Inference Lab. Features:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUnified model catalog\u003c/strong\u003e — all models from all sources in one searchable grid (cloud APIs, custom providers, local Ollama/llama.cpp/GGUF)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePool system\u003c/strong\u003e — Paid, Free, and Local pools with one-click filters\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQuick Add API Key\u003c/strong\u003e — paste any API key (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, etc.), auto-detects provider and discovers models\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLocal LLM scanner\u003c/strong\u003e — auto-detects Ollama models, llama.cpp processes, GGUF files, and HuggingFace cache\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eProvider sidebar\u003c/strong\u003e — see all providers with status dots, model counts, and enable/disable\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLive model status\u003c/strong\u003e — online/offline/running indicators per model\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBuilt-in provider registry\u003c/strong\u003e — DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, Together, OpenRouter, Mistral, Gemini pre-configured\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNavigate to \u003ccode\u003e/lab\u003c/code\u003e or click \"AI Lab Hub\" in the sidebar.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e1. LLM Proxy\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-1-llm-proxy\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 1. LLM Proxy\" href=\"#1-llm-proxy\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eForge Studio receives chat completion requests and routes them to whichever provider has the model you asked for. It supports:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCloud providers\u003c/strong\u003e: Groq, Google Gemini, Mistral, Cerebras, SambaNova, Cohere, OpenRouter, NVIDIA NIM, Cloudflare Workers AI, Together, DeepInfra, Anyscale, Fireworks, Perplexity\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLocal models\u003c/strong\u003e: Ollama, llama.cpp (any GGUF model)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCustom providers\u003c/strong\u003e: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint you paste in\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf a provider goes down, Forge Studio automatically falls back to another provider for the same model. This is handled by a circuit breaker system backed by Redis.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e2. Local Model Load Balancer\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-2-local-model-load-balancer\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 2. Local Model Load Balancer\" href=\"#2-local-model-load-balancer\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGPU-aware model switching with one model active at a time:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUser-selectable\u003c/strong\u003e — choose which model to activate from the UI\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVRAM-aware\u003c/strong\u003e — checks available GPU memory before switching\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eZero-downtime\u003c/strong\u003e — stops current model, starts new one, health-checks before going live\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eReal-time GPU stats\u003c/strong\u003e — VRAM usage, temperature, utilization\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNavigate to \u003ccode\u003e/local-models\u003c/code\u003e in the sidebar.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e3. Custom Providers (Paste-Any-API)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-3-custom-providers-paste-any-api\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 3. Custom Providers (Paste-Any-API)\" href=\"#3-custom-providers-paste-any-api\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGo to the Custom Providers page, paste an API URL and key, and Forge Studio auto-discovers what models are available. This means you can add:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA self-hosted vLLM instance\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAn Azure OpenAI endpoint\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA HuggingFace Inference API\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAny other OpenAI-compatible service\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOnce added, models from that provider appear in your model list and can be used through the standard \u003ccode\u003e/v1/chat/completions\u003c/code\u003e endpoint.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e4. Standalone Mode\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-4-standalone-mode\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 4. Standalone Mode\" href=\"#4-standalone-mode\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eForge Studio can run without LiteLLM. The direct proxy service routes requests straight to providers. LiteLLM is optional - it adds more sophisticated routing and load balancing, but Forge Studio works on its own.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e5. HuggingFace Hub\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-5-huggingface-hub\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 5. HuggingFace Hub\" href=\"#5-huggingface-hub\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSearch for models on HuggingFace directly from Forge Studio. The system:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSearches the HuggingFace API\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eShows model details (size, quantization, downloads)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eChecks your hardware (GPU VRAM, RAM, disk) against model requirements\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEstimates whether the model will run on your machine\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDownloads GGUF files directly to your models directory\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e6. Inference Lab\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-6-inference-lab\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 6. Inference Lab\" href=\"#6-inference-lab\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA chat interface that connects directly to your local backends. You can configure:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGPU layers (ngl) for llama.cpp offloading\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eContext size, batch size, threads\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFlash attention, KV cache quantization\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTemperature, max tokens, top-p\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eReal-time GPU stats (VRAM usage, temperature, tok/s) display during inference.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e7. class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Authenticate (opens browser)\u003c/span\u003e\ncloudflared tunnel login\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Create tunnel\u003c/span\u003e\ncloudflared tunnel create forge-studio\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSSL/TLS → Overview → \u003cstrong\u003eFull (Strict)\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → \u003cstrong\u003eAlways Use HTTPS\u003c/strong\u003e → ON\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → \u003cstrong\u003eHSTS\u003c/strong\u003e → ON\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSecurity → Bots → \u003cstrong\u003eBot Fight Mode\u003c/strong\u003e → ON\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSecurity → WAF → Enable managed rules\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSecurity → Settings → Security Level → \u003cstrong\u003eHigh\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" scanners\n  │\n  ├─ /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible)\n  ├─ /api/stream/chat (SSE streaming)\n  ├─ /api/trpc/* (tRPC for the UI)\n  │\n  ├─ Check custom providers first\n  │   └─ If model matches a custom provider → route directly\n  │\n  └─ Fall back to LiteLLM (if configured)\n      └─ LiteLLM routes to Groq/Gemini/Mistral/etc.\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can use Forge Studio as:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA proxy in front of LiteLLM\u003c/strong\u003e - adds UI, monitoring, budget tracking\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA standalone proxy\u003c/strong\u003e - route directly to providers without LiteLLM\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBoth\u003c/strong\u003e - LiteLLM for complex routing, Forge Studio for the interface\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" tools do one thing: route requests. Forge Studio bundles the full lifecycle:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAI Lab Hub\u003c/strong\u003e: Unified model catalog — one view for all models across cloud APIs, custom providers, local Ollama/llama.cpp, and GGUF files. Quick-add API keys from DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, and more.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLocal Model Switcher\u003c/strong\u003e: GPU-aware model switching with one model active at a time. No more running out of VRAM.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDiscovery\u003c/strong\u003e: Find and pull models from HuggingFace. Auto-detect local LLMs (Ollama, llama.cpp, GGUF).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConfiguration\u003c/strong\u003e: Add providers, set up routing. Paste any OpenAI-compatible API key and it works.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eProxying\u003c/strong\u003e: Route requests with automatic fallback and circuit breaker protection.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eMonitoring\u003c/strong\u003e: See what's happening in real-time with GPU stats, token throughput, and system telemetry.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBudgeting\u003c/strong\u003e: Track and limit spending with per-team monthly budgets and virtual key rate limits.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBuilding\u003c/strong\u003e: Create AI products with the Forge Builder — visual workflow designer with MCP tools.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSecuring\u003c/strong\u003e: Guardrails (PII detection, prompt injection blocking), access control, audit logs.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eReboot-proof\u003c/strong\u003e: PM2 + systemd ensures all services survive reboots. Zero manual intervention.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEverything runs on your hardware. No data leaves your machine unless you configure a cloud provider.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePowering Forge Studio with Your Own API Key\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-powering-forge-studio-with-your-own-api-key\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Powering Forge Studio with Your Own API Key\" href=\"#powering-forge-studio-with-your-own-api-key\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eForge Studio is a proxy. It doesn't have its own AI models. Instead, it uses whatever API keys you configure to power its features:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eChat and Inference\u003c/strong\u003e — Add an OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, or any OpenAI-compatible API key in Custom Providers. Every chat completion, inference lab test, and Forge Builder workflow runs through your configured providers. Your data stays between you and your chosen provider.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSystem Features (No External API Needed)\u003c/strong\u003e — These work entirely locally:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSystem Monitor (CPU/GPU/RAM)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eProcess Manager (PM2)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLocal Model Switcher (GPU load balancer)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLLM Discoverer (Ollama, llama.cpp, GGUF detection)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eHuggingFace Hub (model search, hardware check, download)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBudget tracking, audit logs, access control\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGuardrails\u003c/strong\u003e — PII detection, injection blocking, and toxicity filtering run client-side in the browser. No external API call needed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eForge Builder\u003c/strong\u003e — The workflow builder sends test queries through the same proxy. Use whichever provider you've configured.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe architecture: your API key goes into Forge Studio, Forge Studio routes requests, you see the response. Forge Studio never stores your prompts or responses beyond what's needed for the request log.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="Production-grade AI lab sprint: security hardening + governance + multi-tenancy

Security:
- Added Helmet security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
- Added CORS middleware with ALLOWED_ORIGINS config
- JWT_SECRET now validated at startup (exits if unset)
- Mock auth gated behind NODE_ENV !== production
- Removed hardcoded mock session cookie from /v1/chat/completions proxy

Auth:
- API key auth now validates against virtual keys
- Session-based auth restricted to dev mode
- Removed hardcoded 'local-dev-user' from all auth paths

Multi-tenant:
- Added resolveTeamId() helper to derive team from authenticated user
- Replaced all hardcoded teamId=1 with dynamic team resolution
- All budget checks, request history, and audit logs use correct team

Streaming governance:
- Added budget limit check before processing
- Added pre-call guardrail evaluation (PII, injection blocking)
- Added post-call guardrail evaluation (content safety)
- Added request history + budget spend tracking
- Added audit logging for all streaming completions

Virtual key enforcement:
- TPM/RPM rate limits now enforced with in-memory tracker
- Model access restrictions checked during key validation
- Fixed budget comparison bug (spendUsd was in micro-USD vs dollars)

Dep hygiene:
- Removed stale files: patch_server.py, move_endpoint.py, todo.md, README_FREEAPI.md
- Updated references from freeapi-forge to forge-studio" class="Link--secondary" href="/Jahanzaib211/forge-studio/commit/80bcafe1dc06522acf35ed2096e285f7c17116f7">Production-grade AI lab sprint: ⚡ SYSTEM
- System Monitor: Real-time CPU/GPU/RAM monitoring

Security:
- Added Helmet security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
- Added CORS middleware with ALLOWED_ORIGINS config
- JWT_SECRET now validated at startup (exits if unset)
- Mock auth gated behind NODE_ENV !== production
- Removed hardcoded mock session cookie from /v1/chat/completions proxy

Auth:
- API key auth now validates against virtual keys
- Session-based auth restricted to dev mode
- Removed hardcoded 'local-dev-user' from all auth paths

Multi-tenant:
- Added resolveTeamId() helper to derive team from authenticated user
- Replaced all hardcoded teamId=1 with dynamic team resolution
- All budget checks, request history, and audit logs use correct team

Streaming governance:
- Added budget limit check before processing
- Added pre-call guardrail evaluation (PII, injection blocking)
- Added post-call guardrail evaluation (content safety)
- Added request history + budget spend tracking
- Added audit logging for all streaming completions

Virtual key enforcement:
- TPM/RPM rate limits now enforced with in-memory tracker
- Model access restrictions checked during key validation
- Fixed budget comparison bug (spendUsd was in micro-USD vs dollars)

Dep hygiene:
- Removed stale files: patch_server.py, move_endpoint.py, todo.md, README_FREEAPI.md

Virtual Terminal: Docker execution, xterm.js, WebSocket shell

Backend:
- server/services/sandbox_manager.ts: Docker-based code execution
  with streaming stdout/stderr, 256MB memory limit, network=none,
  CPU cap, 30s default timeout, temp file isolation. Fallback to
  local execution if Docker unavailable. Supports Python, JS,
  TypeScript, Bash, Go.
- server/routers/sandbox_router.ts: tRPC execute/languages/status
  endpoints + WebSocket terminal handler (setupTerminalWebSocket)
  with bidirectional I/O and resize support.
- server/_core/index.ts: Terminal WebSocket on /ws/terminal path,
  separate WSS for code execution shells.
- server/routers.ts: Registered sandbox router

Frontend:
- client/src/components/hub/SandboxPanel.tsx: Slide-up panel with
  Code Runner tab (split-pane editor + output with copy) and
  Terminal tab (xterm.js with FitAddon, real Docker bash shell)
- client/src/pages/ForgeBuilder.tsx: Added [Sandbox] button
  and SandboxPanel integration
- client/src/pages/AILabHub.tsx: [Chat] button opens TestPanel
  inline (no redirect to /dashboard)
- client/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Added '← Back to Hub' button

Dependencies: xterm@5.3.0, @xterm/addon-fit@0.11.0

title="Production-grade AI lab sprint: security hardening + governance + multi-tenancy

Security:
- Added Helmet security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
- Added CORS middleware with ALLOWED_ORIGINS config
- JWT_SECRET now validated at startup (exits if unset)
- Mock auth gated behind NODE_ENV !== production
- Removed hardcoded mock session cookie from /v1/chat/completions proxy

Auth:
- API key auth now validates against virtual keys
- Session-based auth restricted to dev mode
- Removed hardcoded 'local-dev-user' from all auth paths

Multi-tenant:
- Added resolveTeamId() helper to derive team from authenticated user
- Replaced all hardcoded teamId=1 with dynamic team resolution
- All budget checks, request history, and audit logs use correct team

Streaming governance:
- Added budget limit check before processing
- Added pre-call guardrail evaluation (PII, injection blocking)
- Added post-call guardrail evaluation (content safety)
- Added request history + budget spend tracking
- Added audit logging for all streaming completions

Virtual key enforcement:
- TPM/RPM rate limits now enforced with in-memory tracker
- Model access restrictions checked during key validation
- Fixed budget comparison bug (spendUsd was in micro-USD vs dollars)

Dep hygiene:
- Removed stale files: patch_server.py, move_endpoint.py, todo.md, README_FREEAPI.md
- Updated references from freeapi-forge to forge-studio" class="Link--secondary" href="/Jahanzaib211/forge-studio/commit/80bcafe1dc06522acf35ed2096e285f7c17116f7">Production-grade AI lab sprint: <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What is Forge Studio?</h2><a id="user-content-what-is-forge-studio" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What is Forge Studio?" href="#what-is-forge-studio"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio is a self-hosted AI lab control center. It acts as a proxy layer that sits between your applications and LLM providers (OpenAI, Groq, Gemini, Mistral, Ollama, llama.cpp, and others). You configure your API keys once, and Forge Studio handles routing, fallbacks, budget tracking, and monitoring.</p>
<p dir="auto">The key idea: you paste any OpenAI-compatible API URL and key into Forge Studio, and it works. No vendor lock-in. No complex configuration. If it speaks the OpenAI API format, Forge Studio can proxy it.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/Jahanzaib211/forge-studio/blob/main/docs/screenshot-dashboard.png"><img src="/Jahanzaib211/forge-studio/raw/main/docs/screenshot-dashboard.png" alt="Forge Studio Dashboard" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<hr>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What It Actually Does</h2><a id="user-content-what-it-actually-does" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What It Actually Does" href="#what-it-actually-does"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">0. AI Lab Hub — Unified Model Catalog</h3><a id="user-content-0-ai-lab-hub--unified-model-catalog" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 0. AI Lab Hub — Unified Model Catalog" href="#0-ai-lab-hub--unified-model-catalog"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The central command center for all AI models. One page replaces five: Model Manager, Model Explorer, Custom Providers, LLM Discoverer, and Inference Lab. Features:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Unified model catalog</strong> — all models from all sources in one searchable grid (cloud APIs, custom providers, local Ollama/llama.cpp/GGUF)</li>
<li><strong>Pool system</strong> — Paid, Free, and Local pools with one-click filters</li>
<li><strong>Quick Add API Key</strong> — paste any API key (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, etc.), auto-detects provider and discovers models</li>
<li><strong>Local LLM scanner</strong> — auto-detects Ollama models, llama.cpp processes, GGUF files, and HuggingFace cache</li>
<li><strong>Provider sidebar</strong> — see all providers with status dots, model counts, and enable/disable</li>
<li><strong>Live model status</strong> — online/offline/running indicators per model</li>
<li><strong>Built-in provider registry</strong> — DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, Together, OpenRouter, Mistral, Gemini pre-configured</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Navigate to <code>/lab</code> or click "AI Lab Hub" in the sidebar.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. LLM Proxy</h3><a id="user-content-1-llm-proxy" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. LLM Proxy" href="#1-llm-proxy"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio receives chat completion requests and routes them to whichever provider has the model you asked for. It supports:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Cloud providers</strong>: Groq, Google Gemini, Mistral, Cerebras, SambaNova, Cohere, OpenRouter, NVIDIA NIM, Cloudflare Workers AI, Together, DeepInfra, Anyscale, Fireworks, Perplexity</li>
<li><strong>Local models</strong>: Ollama, llama.cpp (any GGUF model)</li>
<li><strong>Custom providers</strong>: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint you paste in</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If a provider goes down, Forge Studio automatically falls back to another provider for the same model. This is handled by a circuit breaker system backed by Redis.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. Local Model Load Balancer</h3><a id="user-content-2-local-model-load-balancer" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. Local Model Load Balancer" href="#2-local-model-load-balancer"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">GPU-aware model switching with one model active at a time:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>User-selectable</strong> — choose which model to activate from the UI</li>
<li><strong>VRAM-aware</strong> — checks available GPU memory before switching</li>
<li><strong>Zero-downtime</strong> — stops current model, starts new one, health-checks before going live</li>
<li><strong>Real-time GPU stats</strong> — VRAM usage, temperature, utilization</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Navigate to <code>/local-models</code> in the sidebar.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">3. Custom Providers (Paste-Any-API)</h3><a id="user-content-3-custom-providers-paste-any-api" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3. Custom Providers (Paste-Any-API)" href="#3-custom-providers-paste-any-api"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Go to the Custom Providers page, paste an API URL and key, and Forge Studio auto-discovers what models are available. This means you can add:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>A self-hosted vLLM instance</li>
<li>An Azure OpenAI endpoint</li>
<li>A HuggingFace Inference API</li>
<li>Any other OpenAI-compatible service</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Once added, models from that provider appear in your model list and can be used through the standard <code>/v1/chat/completions</code> endpoint.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">4. Standalone Mode</h3><a id="user-content-4-standalone-mode" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 4. Standalone Mode" href="#4-standalone-mode"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio can run without LiteLLM. The direct proxy service routes requests straight to providers. LiteLLM is optional - it adds more sophisticated routing and load balancing, but Forge Studio works on its own.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5. HuggingFace Hub</h3><a id="user-content-5-huggingface-hub" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5. HuggingFace Hub" href="#5-huggingface-hub"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Search for models on HuggingFace directly from Forge Studio. The system:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Searches the HuggingFace API</li>
<li>Shows model details (size, quantization, downloads)</li>
<li>Checks your hardware (GPU VRAM, RAM, disk) against model requirements</li>
<li>Estimates whether the model will run on your machine</li>
<li>Downloads GGUF files directly to your models directory</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">6. Inference Lab</h3><a id="user-content-6-inference-lab" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 6. Inference Lab" href="#6-inference-lab"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A chat interface that connects directly to your local backends. You can configure:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>GPU layers (ngl) for llama.cpp offloading</li>
<li>Context size, batch size, threads</li>
<li>Flash attention, KV cache quantization</li>
<li>Temperature, max tokens, top-p</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Real-time GPU stats (VRAM usage, temperature, tok/s) display during inference.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">7. Model Manager</h3><a id="user-content-7-model-manager" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 7. Model Manager" href="#7-model-manager"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Add, remove, and test models in your LiteLLM configuration. Includes:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Quick-add buttons for common free providers</li>
<li>One-click test for each model</li>
<li>Full LiteLLM config viewer</li>
<li>Standalone mode indicator</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">8. System Monitor</h3><a id="user-content-8-system-monitor" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 8. System Monitor" href="#8-system-monitor"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Real-time system monitoring via WebSocket (updates every 2 seconds):</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Per-core CPU usage</li>
<li>RAM and swap usage</li>
<li>GPU utilization, VRAM, temperature, power draw (via nvidia-smi)</li>
<li>Per-process GPU memory attribution</li>
<li>AI process detection (identifies Ollama, llama-server, Python inference processes)</li>
<li>Top processes with kill capability</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">9. Process Manager</h3><a id="user-content-9-process-manager" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 9. Process Manager" href="#9-process-manager"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">View and control PM2 processes from the GUI:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Start, stop, restart, delete processes</li>
<li>View stdout/stderr logs</li>
<li>See CPU, memory, uptime, restart count</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">10. LLM Discoverer</h3><a id="user-content-10-llm-discoverer" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 10. LLM Discoverer" href="#10-llm-discoverer"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Automatically detects locally available models:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Ollama models (via <code>ollama list</code> and <code>ollama ps</code>)</li>
<li>llama.cpp servers (from running processes)</li>
<li>GGUF files on disk</li>
<li>HuggingFace cache</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">11. Forge Builder</h3><a id="user-content-11-forge-builder" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 11. Forge Builder" href="#11-forge-builder"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A workflow builder for creating AI products. You can:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Add workflow blocks (system prompt, model selection, tool config, code blocks)</li>
<li>Connect to MCP servers for external tools</li>
<li>Test the workflow with streaming output</li>
<li>Deploy as an agent</li>
<li>Save/load projects from localStorage</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">12. MCP Integration</h3><a id="user-content-12-mcp-integration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 12. MCP Integration" href="#12-mcp-integration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>As MCP Host</strong>: Connect to external MCP servers to use their tools in your workflows.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>As MCP Server</strong>: Forge Studio exposes its own capabilities (chat completion, model listing, system stats) as MCP tools at <code>/mcp/sse</code>.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">21. Browser Extension</h3><a id="user-content-21-browser-extension" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 21. Browser Extension" href="#21-browser-extension"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A Chrome/Edge extension that sends chat completions directly from your browser toolbar. Configure your API key and endpoint, then use <code>Ctrl+Enter</code> to send messages from any page. Supports all task types (chat, coding, vision, fast, long context).</p>
<p dir="auto">See the <a href="/Jahanzaib211/forge-studio/blob/main/extension/README.md">extension README</a> for installation and usage.</p>
<hr>

<tr>
<th>Layer</th>
<th>What</th>
<th>Why</th>
</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

<td>Process Manager</td>
<td>PM2</td>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Running It</h2><a id="user-content-running-it" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Running It" href="#running-it"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Default login</strong>: The seed script creates an admin user at <code>admin@forge.local</code>. The app uses a mock session — once you visit the page, you're in. Change this before exposing to others.</p>

<p dir="auto">The installer:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Detects your OS and installs missing dependencies</li>
<li>Clones the repo, installs and builds</li>
<li>Sets up PostgreSQL, Redis, Qdrant</li>
<li>Configures PM2 (reboot-proof)</li>
<li>Sets up nginx reverse proxy</li>
<li>Opens the browser</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Reboot-Proof Setup</h3><a id="user-content-reboot-proof-setup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Reboot-Proof Setup" href="#reboot-proof-setup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio uses PM2 for process management with systemd integration:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install PM2 globally</span>
npm install -g pm2

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Start forge-studio in production mode</span>
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> forge-studio
pm2 start ecosystem.production.cjs

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Save process list (survives reboots)</span>
pm2 save

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Set up systemd auto-start (needs sudo once)</span>
sudo env PATH=<span class="pl-smi">$PATH</span> pm2 startup systemd -u <span class="pl-smi">$USER</span> --hp <span class="pl-smi">$HOME</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Install PM2 globally
npm install -g pm2

# Start forge-studio in production mode
cd forge-studio
pm2 start ecosystem.production.cjs

# Save process list (survives reboots)
pm2 save

# Set up systemd auto-start (needs sudo once)
sudo env PATH=$PATH pm2 startup systemd -u $USER --hp $HOME" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cloudflare Tunnel (Remote Access)</h3><a id="user-content-cloudflare-tunnel-remote-access" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cloudflare Tunnel (Remote Access)" href="#cloudflare-tunnel-remote-access"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Expose your local Forge Studio to the internet via Cloudflare Tunnel:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install cloudflared</span>
curl -fsSL https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/releases/latest/download/cloudflared-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/cloudflared
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cloudflared

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Authenticate (opens browser)</span>
cloudflared tunnel login

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Create tunnel</span>
cloudflared tunnel create forge-studio

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Configure</span>
bash scripts/setup-cloudflare-tunnel.sh

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Start via PM2</span>
pm2 start cloudflared --name cloudflared -- tunnel run forge-studio</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Install cloudflared
curl -fsSL https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflared/releases/latest/download/cloudflared-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/cloudflared
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cloudflared

# Authenticate (opens browser)
cloudflared tunnel login

# Create tunnel
cloudflared tunnel create forge-studio

# Configure
bash scripts/setup-cloudflare-tunnel.sh

# Start via PM2
pm2 start cloudflared --name cloudflared -- tunnel run forge-studio" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Then in Cloudflare Dashboard:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>SSL/TLS → Overview → <strong>Full (Strict)</strong></li>
<li>SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → <strong>Always Use HTTPS</strong> → ON</li>
<li>SSL/TLS → Edge Certificates → <strong>HSTS</strong> → ON</li>
<li>Security → Bots → <strong>Bot Fight Mode</strong> → ON</li>
<li>Security → WAF → Enable managed rules</li>
<li>Security → Settings → Security Level → <strong>High</strong></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Nginx Reverse Proxy</h3><a id="user-content-nginx-reverse-proxy" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Nginx Reverse Proxy" href="#nginx-reverse-proxy"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The included nginx config (<code>nginx/forge-studio.conf</code>) handles:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Rate limiting (100 req/min API, 30 req/min streaming)</li>
<li>WebSocket upgrade for <code>/ws</code></li>
<li>SSE proxy for <code>/api/stream</code> and <code>/mcp/sse</code></li>
<li>Security headers (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, CSP)</li>
<li>Gzip compression</li>
</ul>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>sudo cp nginx/forge-studio.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/forge-studio
sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/forge-studio /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo nginx -t <span class="pl-k">&&</span> sudo systemctl reload nginx</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="sudo cp nginx/forge-studio.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/forge-studio
sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/forge-studio /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Forge Studio works as both an MCP client and server.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">As MCP Server</h3><a id="user-content-as-mcp-server" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: As MCP Server" href="#as-mcp-server"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio exposes its capabilities as MCP tools. Any MCP-compatible client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) can connect:</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Endpoint</strong>: <code>http://localhost:5051/mcp/sse</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Available tools</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>chat_completion</code> - Send a chat message</li>
<li><code>list_models</code> - List available models</li>
<li><code>get_system_stats</code> - Get CPU/GPU/RAM stats</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Connect from Claude Desktop</strong> (claude_desktop_config.json):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"mcpServers"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"forge-studio"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"url"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>http://localhost:5051/mcp/sse<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
    }
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">You can use Forge Studio as:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>A proxy in front of LiteLLM</strong> - adds UI, monitoring, budget tracking</li>
<li><strong>A standalone proxy</strong> - route directly to providers without LiteLLM</li>
<li><strong>Both</strong> - LiteLLM for complex routing, Forge Studio for the interface</li>
</ol>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Makes It Different</h2><a id="user-content-what-makes-it-different" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Makes It Different" href="#what-makes-it-different"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Most LLM tools do one thing: route requests. Forge Studio bundles the full lifecycle:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>AI Lab Hub</strong>: Unified model catalog — one view for all models across cloud APIs, custom providers, local Ollama/llama.cpp, and GGUF files. Quick-add API keys from DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, and more.</li>
<li><strong>Local Model Switcher</strong>: GPU-aware model switching with one model active at a time. No more running out of VRAM.</li>
<li><strong>Discovery</strong>: Find and pull models from HuggingFace. Auto-detect local LLMs (Ollama, llama.cpp, GGUF).</li>
<li><strong>Configuration</strong>: Add providers, set up routing. Paste any OpenAI-compatible API key and it works.</li>
<li><strong>Proxying</strong>: Route requests with automatic fallback and circuit breaker protection.</li>
<li><strong>Monitoring</strong>: See what's happening in real-time with GPU stats, token throughput, and system telemetry.</li>
<li><strong>Budgeting</strong>: Track and limit spending with per-team monthly budgets and virtual key rate limits.</li>
<li><strong>Building</strong>: Create AI products with the Forge Builder — visual workflow designer with MCP tools.</li>
<li><strong>Securing</strong>: Guardrails (PII detection, prompt injection blocking), access control, audit logs.</li>
<li><strong>Reboot-proof</strong>: PM2 + systemd ensures all services survive reboots. Zero manual intervention.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Everything runs on your hardware. No data leaves your machine unless you configure a cloud provider.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Powering Forge Studio with Your Own API Key</h2><a id="user-content-powering-forge-studio-with-your-own-api-key" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Powering Forge Studio with Your Own API Key" href="#powering-forge-studio-with-your-own-api-key"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Forge Studio is a proxy. It doesn't have its own AI models. Instead, it uses whatever API keys you configure to power its features:</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Chat and Inference</strong> — Add an OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, or any OpenAI-compatible API key in Custom Providers. Every chat completion, inference lab test, and Forge Builder workflow runs through your configured providers. Your data stays between you and your chosen provider.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>System Features (No External API Needed)</strong> — These work entirely locally:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>System Monitor (CPU/GPU/RAM)</li>
<li>Process Manager (PM2)</li>
<li>Local Model Switcher (GPU load balancer)</li>
<li>LLM Discoverer (Ollama, llama.cpp, GGUF detection)</li>
<li>HuggingFace Hub (model search, hardware check, download)</li>
<li>Budget tracking, audit logs, access control</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Guardrails</strong> — PII detection, injection blocking, and toxicity filtering run client-side in the browser. No external API call needed.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Forge Builder</strong> — The workflow builder sends test queries through the same proxy. Use whichever provider you've configured.</p>
<p dir="auto">The architecture: your API key goes into Forge Studio, Forge Studio routes requests, you see the response. Forge Studio never stores your prompts or responses beyond what's needed for the request log.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">License</h2><a id="user-content-license" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: License" href="#license"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
